title: RegExp literal with noLiterals (degrades to RegExp)
notes: `{noLiterals: true}` degrades the literal to its base type `RegExp`. Any RegExp instance passes (constructor form `new RegExp(...)` included); source + flags are no longer matched.
pureType:
const reg = /abc/i;
return createValidate<typeof reg>(undefined, {noLiterals: true});
